evidence: approximately $1,850 for the acquisition of CentralReach, a leading provider of SaaS and AI-enabled solutions for applied behavior analysis therapy clinicians
approximately $800 for the acquisition of Subsplash, a leading provider of AI-enabled SaaS, and integrated giving solutions, for faith-based organizations
In the last three years, we have deployed approximately $8,960 of capital toward acquisitions

Increasingly, this includes AI-enabled products and functionality embedded within customers mission-critical workflows... we believe these AI capabilities enhance product differentiation, and drive incremental automation and improved customer outcomes

Aderant comprehensive management software and AI-enabled solutions for law and other professional services firms... Deltek enterprise software, SaaS, and AI-enabled information solutions for government contractors
DAT... AI-enabled analytics solutions... iPipeline cloud-based software and AI-enabled analytics solutions for the life insurance/annuities and financial services industries

We rely on third-party AI platforms and services, including proprietary and open-source large language models... providers may change their terms of service, increase pricing, discontinue services... our exposure to pricing changes from these providers increases

We face emerging risks from AI-powered attacks, including deepfakes used to impersonate executives or customers, AI-assisted social engineering, prompt injection attempts against AI systems, and data poisoning targeting machine learning models

The EU AI Act classifies AI systems by risk level and may prohibit certain high-risk applications, requiring significant change to product design, documentation, governance processes, and risk management practices

Total capital deployed over last three years toward acquisitions, several of which are AI-enabled

limitations: AI investment intensity is driven primarily by acquisition spend on AI-enabled targets; no standalone AI R&D or infrastructure capex figures are disclosed separately, so the $8,960 total acquisition figure is not fully AI-attributable.

Narrative concreteness scores 4 rather than 5 because no quantified business results (%, $, time saved) from deployed AI features are provided in the excerpts; descriptions are operational but not outcome-quantified.

Risk disclosure depth scores 4 rather than 5 because no quantified risk exposure figures or named internal AI governance oversight bodies are cited in the excerpts; mechanisms are described but not quantified.
